mnemonics: HORSE: Horspool's Orderly Rightmost Shift Estimation
BAD BOYS: Boyer-Moore uses Bad character and Good suffix rules
keyPoints: Input enhancement preprocesses the pattern (or text) to gather information that speeds up the later matching process.
The Boyer-Moore algorithm uses two heuristics for shifting: the bad-character shift and the good-suffix shift.
Horspool's algorithm is a simplified version of Boyer-Moore that uses only the bad-character heuristic for a more practical and often faster average-case performance.
commonMistakes: Confusing the direction of the shift in the bad-character rule (you shift the pattern to align the rightmost occurrence of that character in the pattern *to the left of the current mismatch* with the text's character).
Forgetting that preprocessing the pattern is the core 'input enhancement' step that makes these algorithms efficient, and assuming the entire text is preprocessed instead.
